flashcards.back: A way of writing very large or small numbers as a coefficient (between 1 and 10) multiplied by a power of 10 ($a \times 10^n$).

flashcards.front: What is the restriction on the coefficient $a$ in scientific notation?
flashcards.back: $a$ must be greater than or equal to 1, and strictly less than 10 ($1 \le a < 10$).

flashcards.front: What does a positive exponent in scientific notation indicate?
flashcards.back: A large number (the decimal point was shifted to the left).

flashcards.front: What does a negative exponent in scientific notation indicate?
flashcards.back: A small decimal number between 0 and 1 (the decimal point was shifted to the right).
$420,000$ (move the decimal point 5 places to the right).
